title: Is Black Seed Oil the new superfood?

chunks.title: Introduction to Black Seed Oil
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses black seed oil, addressing its reputation as a 'cure-all' and emphasizing that it should be considered medicine rather than food. They mention that compounds in black seed oil can have physiological effects and provide some potential benefits in specific contexts, but they caution against overestimating its value in a healthy diet.
chunks.transcript: what about black seed oil paul this is from andrew he says i've heard this is a cure-all does it have any place in a healthy human diet to which i would respond medicine not food i have no issue with the notion that there are plant derived compounds nigella sativa is the seed that is used to make black seed oil thymoquinone is one of the compounds found in black seed oil that appears to have physiologic effects in humans i have no issue with the fact that compounds found in plants affect the human body physiologically many of our medicines many of our pharmaceuticals in western medicine are derived from plants but we don't think of them as food or vitamins black seed

chunks.title: Potential Benefits and Side Effects
chunks.summary: The speaker elaborates on the physiological effects of black seed oil, particularly its ability to lower blood sugar, which can be harmful for healthy individuals. They also discuss other drugs like metformin and berberine, exploring their side effects and questioning the safety of such compounds for general health.
chunks.transcript: you don't need black seed oil does black seed oil have some data that it could be helpful in certain situations yes it does and it may be because it acts as a pro-oxidant triggering the glutathione system in people who are eating a traditional shitty diet and have lots of inflammation or oxidative stress just like this gonna trigger nrf2 in the same way that many other plant compounds will doesn't mean they're antioxidants means they're pro oxidants but as we've seen with other compounds like sulforaphane or plant compounds we must not ignore the side effects that is what everyone seems to be doing with flaxseed black seed oil now we're just all excited about this new quote-unquote superfood that's going to cure everything and we're ignoring the side effects what are the side effects of black seed oil well i would say one of the side effects is that it lowers your blood sugar this is something that's been touted as a benefit but i don't think that if people have high blood sugar you give them something that lowers the blood sugar this is my problem with metformin which has other negative side effects if you listen to the podcast a little bit derek for more plates more dates we talked about males who have taken metformin having offspring male offspring with increased incidence of penile defects specifically hypospadias very interesting study that just came out metformin is going to have side effects berberine is going to have side effects metformin also inhibits many compounds many enzymatic systems in the human body causes lactic acidosis b12 deficiency it's not a vitamin it doesn't the whole longevity metformin thing is absolutely absurd that is sacrificing so much i would say prosperity today for an empty promise or a potentially empty

chunks.title: Hormonal Disruptions and Risks
chunks.summary: The discussion turns to the hormonal effects of black seed oil, highlighting findings that suggest it may disrupt hormone levels and potentially lead to reproductive issues. The speaker stresses the need for further research on the hormonal implications of black seed oil and advises against using it to manage diabetes.

chunks.title: Comparison with Curcumin
chunks.summary: The speaker compares black seed oil to curcumin, another plant-based remedy, noting that while curcumin may have some benefits, its negative effects on testosterone and other health markers should not be overlooked. They emphasize both substances are more like medicine with potential drawbacks rather than essential dietary components.
chunks.transcript: something that needs to be discovered but people may say great there's testosterone well there's a heck of a lot more estradiol in nigella sativa than there is testosterone so this is the type of thing that i worry about it's medicine not food understand what we are using this for what are the potential benefits what are the potential risks and do you even need this in your diet it's not going to make you better if you're already healthy and eating an animal-based diet in my opinion it's it's medicine not food just like turmeric yeah turmeric might have some data showing that chunks.title: Animal-Based Diet Philosophy
chunks.summary: The speaker advocates for an animal-based diet, asserting that the optimal foods for human health are meat, organs, honey, raw dairy, and fruits. They argue that vegetables are overrated, potentially harmful to health, and do not contribute positively to human nutrition, promoting a focus on nutrient-dense animal products instead.
chunks.transcript: believe that black seed oil is something that normally healthy humans should be taking on a daily basis understand that there are negative side effects to all of these plants because they are in fact defending themselves as all plants do with their seeds this is a seed this is the first principle that i'm trying to communicate to you guys with an animal-based diet leaves and stems and roots and seeds will have defense chemicals be very careful not to ignore the side effects associated with them i don't think you need any of them in your diet to be optimal i think the optimal foods for humans are the foods we intuitively seek first meat and organs get them either fresh or desiccated like we make it hard in soil fruit honey raw dairy kefir i'll show you how to make it that's what you need to be healthy
